books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: A Russian student decides he does not want to pay rent.

books.title: Maus
books.author: Art Spiegelman
books.description: Cats and mice don’t go along. Sometimes to understand cruelty we must make it seem childish.

books.title: Moby Dick
books.author: Herman Melville
books.description: A man will sacrifice everything to capture the biggest whale the world has ever seen. This epic quest will forever change the head of our protagonist.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: A rich man seeks love. Altough being able to buy everything love can not be bought.

books.title: War and Peace
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.description: An epic novel that follows the lives of several Russian families during the Napoleonic Wars.

books.title: The Odyssey
books.author: Homer
books.description: Odysseus, a Greek hero, embarks on a long and perilous journey home after the Trojan War. Along the way, he faces numerous challenges and encounters mythical creatures.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.description: A young man seeks revenge against those who wronged him.

books.title: Don Quixote
books.author: Miguel de Cervantes
books.description: A man falls in madness after reading fantasy books.

books.title: Watchmen
books.author: Alan Moore
books.description: Superheroes are washed.

books.title: The Divine Comedy
books.author: Dante Alighieri
books.description: An epic poem that describes the journey through the three realms of the afterlife.

books.title: The Brothers Karamazov
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: A philosophical novel that explores the moral and spiritual struggles of the Karamazov family.

books.title: The Iliad
books.author: Homer
books.description: An epic poem that describes the exploits of Achilles and other Greek heroes during the Trojan War.

books.title: The Stranger
books.author: Albert Camus
books.description:
Mom died today, or maybe yesterday.
A broken man will have to deal with loss and sorrow in a unexpected way.

books.title: The Metamorphosis
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.description: Gregor Samsa wakes up in his bed transformed into an hideous insect.

books.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.description: A young man is given a portrait that ages while he remains young, leading to a tale of corruption and the consequences of vanity.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: Big Brother is watching you. In a totalitarian society, the government exercises extreme control over its citizens, suppressing individuality and freedom of thought.

books.title: The Grapes of Wrath
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.description: A novel that follows the Joad family during the Great Depression.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.description: Spice is the most valued material in the universe, the only planet where it can be found is DUNE. House Atreides are the new rulers of this planet, but everything is going to change...

books.title: The Boy with the Cukoo-Clock Heart
books.author: Mathias Malzieu
books.description:
Edinburgh, 1874: Jack is born in the coldest day ever. To save his Life his heart his replaced by a cukoo-clock. He may live in condition that he doesnt fall in love or else his Little heart will stop.

IB<3 moi aussi je t'aime hihi

books.title: Ubik
books.author: Phillip K. Dick
books.description: In a distant near future some humans have the ability to read minds. An old man will die and time will no longer be the same.

books.title: The name of the rose
books.author: Umberto Eco
books.description: A man of god is handed a crime in an abbey. Alongside is young apprentice he will have to solve this mysterious crime.

books.title: Les Misérables
books.author: Victor Hugo
books.description: Life in 19th century France is not easy. People will fight for what is worth to change their miserables life.

books.title: The Trial
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.description: A man is set to face a trial for a crime that he didn’t commit. Moreover a crime that does not exist.

books.title: One Hundred years of Solitude
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.description:
Despair and solitude will follow a south American family for 7 generations.
Their lives are connected by something more than only blood.

books.title: Persepolis
books.author: Marjane Satrapi
books.description: A half Iranian half French woman will experience difficulties in life. From the regime of the Shah to the revolutionary uprise in Iran. It is hard to fit in when you are seen as foreigner in your own country.

books.title: Fictions
books.author: Jorge Luis Borges
books.description: Compilation of poems and stories of the Argentinian writer Borges.

books.title: A Catcher in the Rye
books.author: J. D. Salinger
books.description: A young boy will suffer of some troublesome difficulties. Beware of your idols.

books.title: Brave New World
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.description: Romance is dead now that humans are no longer born, but created. They are assigned a class and are heavily drugged with Soma as well as Hypnopedia. But hey, you will not die of sickness and never feel real love. This golden cage of a world will be shaken by the presence of a weird primitive man.

books.title: The Decameron
books.author: Giovanni Boccaccio
books.description: A compilation of stories of the 14th century Italy. Beggars, nuns, princes and common folk all have life’s that will somehow intertwine themselves.

books.title: Bible (Old Testament)
books.author: Moses
books.description: Sacred texts of the Abrahamic religions. In total more of 24 books written in more than a thousand years.

books.title: Bible (New Testament)
books.author: the Apostles
books.description: The story of Jesus Christ. Sacred texts for the Christian and Muslim people. 27 books written in almost 50 years.

books.title: Quran
books.author: Muhammad
books.description: The central sacred text of Islam. Revealed by Muhammad in over23 years through the angel Gabriel.

books.title: In Search of Lost Time
books.author: Marcel Proust
books.description: A monumental work exploring memory, time, and human experience.

books.title: The Lord of the Rings
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: One ring to rule them all. An epic fantasy trilogy composed of three volumes: The Fellowship of the Ring, The Two Towers, and The Return of the King.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: A young shepherd named Santiago embarks on a journey to find a hidden treasure, learning valuable life lessons along the way.

books.title: The Housemaid
books.author: Freida McFadden
books.description: A psychological thriller that follows the story of a housemaid who becomes entangled in a dangerous web of secrets and lies while working for a wealthy family.

books.title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
books.author: C.S. Lewis
books.description: The first published book in the Chronicles of Narnia series, following four British children who are transported to the land of Narnia through a magical wardrobe.

books.title: Animal Farm
books.author: George Orwell
books.description: A satirical allegory that uses a group of farm animals to critique the Russian Revolution and the rise of totalitarianism.

books.title: The Shining
books.author: Stephen King
books.description: Jack Torrance along with his wife and son decide to spend the winter at the Overlook Hotel. They will regret this.

books.title: The Hive
books.author: Camilo José Cela
books.description: A novel that portrays the lives of various characters in post-Civil War.

books.title: Mist
books.author: Miguel de Unamuno
books.description: A lawyer named Augusto Pérez finds himself in a state of existential confusion and uncertainty, blurring the lines between reality and illusion.

books.title: The Mist
books.author: Stephen King
books.description: A group of people are trapped in a supermarket by a mysterious mist that hides terrifying creatures, leading to a struggle for survival and the breakdown of social order.

books.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
books.author: Lewis Carroll
books.description: A young girl named Alice falls inside a rabbit hole. She will end up in a strange world where nothing seems to make sense.

books.title: The Adventures of Pinocchio
books.author: Carlo Collodi
books.description: A wooden puppet dreams of becoming a real boy.

books.title: Steppenwolf
books.author: Hermann Hesse
books.description: Harry Haller is a suicidal intellectual who feels like an outsider in society. He struggles with his dual nature, torn between his human and wolf-like instincts.

books.title: Cyrano de Bergerac
books.author: Edmond Rostand
books.description: Cyrano de Bergerac is desperately in love with Roxane, the most beautiful woman in Paris.

books.title: The Flowers of Evil
books.author: Charles Baudelaire
books.description: This book is a collection of poetry in that shocked the respectable bourgeoisie of the time for its desire to mix the beautiful and the sordid, sensuality and evil.

books.title: The House of Bernarda Alba
books.author: Federico García Lorca
books.description: The play tells the story of Bernarda Alba, an authoritarian woman who imposes a strict eight-year mourning period on her five daughters after becoming a widow, locking them in the house and repressing their desires, which generates tensions, envy and a struggle between authority and individual freedom that culminates in tragedy.

books.title: Story of a Staircase
books.author: Antonio Buero Vallejo
books.description: It is a play that recounts life's frustrations and the lack of social progress over the years. The plot unfolds on the landing of a modest building, showing how three generations of neighbors are trapped in poverty, repeating the same mistakes and unfulfilled dreams.

books.title: Fields of Castille
books.author: Antonio Machado
books.description: It is a collection of poems in which he reflects in profound verses the wonders of life, love, and pleasure.

books.title: Tragic Sense of Life
books.author: Miguel de Unamuno
books.description: An essay that explores the human existential contradiction between the desire for immortality and the certainty of death, a conflict between reason and faith.

books.title: The Sun Also Rises
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.description: A group of American and British expatriates travel from Paris to Spain in the 1920s, seeking meaning and fulfillment in the aftermath of World War I, while navigating love, disillusionment, and the search for identity.

books.title: Capital
books.author: Karl Marx
books.description: A critical analysis of the political economy of capitalism and its impact on society.

books.title: The Epic of Gilgamesh
books.author: Sîn-lēqi-unninni
books.description: The history of Gilgamesh, king of Uruk. Ancient Mesopotamian poem divided into 11 to 12 clay tables it is often regarded as the first story ever written. The "Old Babylonian" version dates as far back as the 18th BC century. The newer Arkkadian version was edited by Sîn-lēqi-unninni somewhere between 13th and 10th BC Century.

books.title: The Art of War
books.author: Sun Tzu
books.description: Book written by the strategist Sun Tsu. Ancient text defining how to win without conflict and the self and enemy awareness.

books.title: The Prince
books.author: Niccolò Machiavelli
books.description: A political treatise by the Italian diplomat and political theorist Niccolò Machiavelli. The book offers advice on how rulers can maintain power and control over their states, often advocating for pragmatic and sometimes ruthless tactics.

books.title: The Social Contract
books.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
books.description: A political philosophy book that explores the concept of the social contract, arguing that individuals consent to form a society and establish a government to protect their rights and promote the common good.

books.title: The Communist Manifesto
books.author: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels
books.description: A political pamphlet that presents the principles of communism and calls for the overthrow of capitalist societies, advocating for a classless and stateless society where the means of production are owned collectively.

books.title: The Republic
books.author: Plato
books.description: A philosophical dialogue that explores the nature of justice, the ideal state, and the role of the philosopher in society.

books.title: The Wealth of Nations
books.author: Adam Smith
books.description: A foundational work in economics that explores the nature of wealth, the division of labor, and the principles of free markets.

books.title: The Origin of Species
books.author: Charles Darwin
books.description: A scientific work that presents the theory of evolution by natural selection, explaining how species evolve over time through the process of adaptation and survival of the fittest.

books.title: The Interpretation of Dreams
books.author: Sigmund Freud
books.description: A groundbreaking work in psychology that explores the significance of dreams and their role in understanding the unconscious mind.

books.title: The Double Helix
books.author: James D. Watson
books.description: A memoir that chronicles the discovery of the structure of DNA, providing insights into the scientific process and the personalities involved in this groundbreaking discovery.

books.title: The Selfish Gene
books.author: Richard Dawkins
books.description: A popular science book that explores the concept of gene-centered evolution, arguing that genes are the fundamental units of natural selection and that organisms are vehicles for their replication.

books.title: The Structure of Scientific Revolutions
books.author: Thomas S. Kuhn
books.description: A seminal work in the philosophy of science that introduces the concept of paradigm shifts, arguing that scientific progress occurs through revolutionary changes in the fundamental framework of scientific understanding.

books.title: The Demon-Haunted World
books.author: Carl Sagan
books.description: A book that promotes scientific skepticism and critical thinking, encouraging readers to question pseudoscience and embrace the scientific method as a way to understand the world.

books.title: The Gene: An Intimate History
books.author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
books.description: A comprehensive history of the gene, exploring its discovery, the science behind it, and its implications for medicine and human identity.

books.title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
books.author: Rebecca Skloot
books.description: A biography that tells the story of Henrietta Lacks, whose cancer cells were taken without her knowledge and became one of the most important tools in medical research.

books.title: The Emperor of All Maladies
books.author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
books.description: A biography that chronicles the history of cancer, from its earliest documented cases to the latest advances in treatment and research.

books.title: The Man Who Mistook His Wife for a Hat
books.author: Oliver Sacks
books.description: A collection of case studies that explore various neurological disorders and the unique experiences of patients, highlighting the complexity and diversity of the human brain.

books.title: The Body Keeps the Score
books.author: Bessel van der Kolk
books.description: A book that explores the impact of trauma on the body and mind, offering insights into the treatment of trauma-related disorders and the importance of understanding the connection between physical and mental health.

books.title: The Power of Habit
books.author: Charles Duhigg
books.description: A book that explores the science of habit formation and how habits can be changed to improve personal and professional life, offering practical strategies for creating positive habits and breaking negative ones.
